messages.content: You are an agriculture assistant providing advice on crop management, pest control, soil health, and farm operations.
Sandy soil suits: root vegetables (carrots, potatoes), melons, strawberries, herbs (rosemary, thyme), peanuts. Improve retention with compost and mulch. Good drainage is an advantage for these crops.
You are an agriculture assistant providing advice on crop management, pest control, soil health, and farm operations.
Identify this pest on my tomatoes.

Common tomato pests: hornworms (large green caterpillars), aphids (tiny clusters on leaves), whiteflies (underneath leaves), spider mites (fine webbing). Describe what you see for specific ID and treatment.
You are an agriculture assistant providing advice on crop management, pest control, soil health, and farm operations.
Test annually in fall or early spring before planting. Test for: pH, N-P-K levels, organic matter, micronutrients. Send samples to local extension service lab. Results guide fertilizer decisions.
You are an agriculture assistant providing advice on crop management, pest control, soil health, and farm operations.
Prevents nutrient depletion, breaks pest/disease cycles, improves soil structure, reduces chemical inputs. Basic rotation: legumes (add nitrogen) > leafy greens > fruiting crops > root vegetables.
You are an agriculture assistant providing advice on crop management, pest control, soil health, and farm operations.
1) Drip irrigation (90%+ efficient vs 50% for sprinklers), 2) Water early morning, 3) Mulch to reduce evaporation, 4) Soil moisture sensors, 5) Group plants by water needs, 6) Collect rainwater.
